chapter_goal: Orient the second volume around the three-way interaction of topology, variation of Hodge structure, and algebraic cycles.
checks: all three volume themes have chapter coverage
volume I prerequisite nodes are connected to volume II applications
lab: Pick a later theorem and trace which side of the triangle supplies its input.
library_routes: NetworkX for dependency edges across the volume
pitfalls: forgetting which results are from compact Kahler geometry and which require projectivity
treating the three parts as independent
using mixed Hodge vocabulary without tracking both filtrations
proof_moves: reuse volume I as a toolkit
compare hyperplane-section topology with Hodge-theoretic decomposition
visual_focus: A topology-VHS-cycles triangle marks how the second volume routes results through Lefschetz and Leray mechanisms.
